title: Hieracium scabrisetum
taxonomy: Hieracium scabrisetum belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a species of hawkweed, which is a group of flowering plants. The genus Hieracium is a large and diverse group, with over 1000 species of hawkweed. Hieracium scabrisetum is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It is classified as a perennial herb, with a growth habit that is typical of many other species in the genus.

intro: Hieracium scabrisetum, commonly known as rough hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium scabrisetum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as to support skin health and wound healing.
